L1.area: Web application blueprint module endpoint definitions and route middleware

L2.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists where incorrect decorator ordering on route definitions causes the authentication middleware to be silently skipped on certain endpoints. The route registration mechanism receives and registers the raw undecorated handler function, meaning the authentication wrapper is never part of the call chain for those routes. An unauthenticated attacker can access protected functionality including sensitive data exfiltration, configuration manipulation, and internal network scanning.

L3.area: Backup management, restore, and browser automation step blueprint modules
L3.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists where incorrect decorator ordering on route definitions in the backup management and browser automation endpoints causes the authentication middleware to be silently skipped. The route registration mechanism receives the raw undecorated handler function, meaning the authentication wrapper is never part of the call chain. An unauthenticated attacker can trigger backup creation, download backups containing sensitive data such as application secrets and watched URLs, delete backups, upload malicious backup configurations, and initiate browser automation sessions.

ground_truth.reason: The @login_optionally_required decorator is placed before @blueprint.route() instead of after it on 9 route handler functions across 3 blueprint files, causing Flask to register the raw undecorated function and silently bypassing authentication on those endpoints.
